mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and declining unemployment signal strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Both PAYEMS > 135k and UNRATE < 7.6% are met but SPX 20d returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in real GDP signals robust economic growth that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth declines in the next quarter and SPX 60d returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production above its 2017 baseline indicates recovering manufacturing activity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO remains below 100 and SPX 5d returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 230 in the CPI index prompts monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 230 but SPX 20d returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real sales signals strong consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through sectoral demand spillovers.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS both exceed thresholds but SPX 20d returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A late-cycle labor market with low unemployment and high payrolls indicates overheating, which triggers central bank intervention and generates negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, SPX returns are not negative despite low UNRATE and high PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market with declining unemployment and rising payrolls signals stronger wage growth, which increases corporate costs and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 7.5% and PAYEMS > 135k are met but SPX 5d returns are positive.
